title: IPv4 address is assigned automatically inside container when start the container with only ipv4 address specified
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**
I created the dual stack macvlan. While starting the container, only specifiy the ipv4 address, however, the IPv6 address is assigned automatically.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Create dual stack macvlan with the following command:
[root@centos8 bin]# ./podman network create -d macvlan -o parent=ens192 --subnet=192.168.1.10/24 --gateway=192.168.1.1 --subnet=2630:210:f021:9011::/64 --gateway=2630:210:f021:9011::1 ens192
ens192
2. Inspect the network:
[root@centos8 bin]# ./podman inspect ens192
[
     {
          "name": "ens192",
          "id": "22e496b0a5ffb7abbe918389cdbad3e10e9961f562b4c3e59cd8bfb50773d055",
          "driver": "macvlan",
          "network_interface": "ens192",
          "created": "2022-04-02T02:09:38.745993904+08:00",
          "subnets": [
               {
                    "subnet": "192.168.1.0/24",
                    "gateway": "192.168.1.1"
               },
               {
                    "subnet": "2630:210:f021:9011::/64",
                    "gateway": "2630:210:f021:9011::1"
               }
          ],
          "ipv6_enabled": true,
          "internal": false,
          "dns_enabled": false,
          "ipam_options": {
               "driver": "host-local"
          }
     }
]
3. Start a container with only IPv4 address specified 
[root@centos8 bin]# docker image ls
Emulate Docker CLI using podman. Create /etc/containers/nodocker to quiet msg.
REPOSITORY                TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED       SIZE
localhost/netbackup/main  9.1         32572d8c0408  7 months ago  5.81 GB
[root@centos8 bin]# docker ps -a
Emulate Docker CLI using podman. Create /etc/containers/nodocker to quiet msg.
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
[root@centos8 bin]# ./podman run -itd --name=master --network=ens192 **--ip=192.168.1.5** 32572d8c0408 /bin/bash
0863497a57ceee26362efae0cd5d85c497943a009193244761807341723b20ce
[root@centos8 bin]# docker ps -a
Emulate Docker CLI using podman. Create /etc/containers/nodocker to quiet msg.
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                         COMMAND     CREATED        STATUS            PORTS       NAMES
0863497a57ce  localhost/netbackup/main:9.1  /bin/bash   9 seconds ago  Up 6 seconds ago              master

**Describe the results you received:**
IPv6 address is assigned automatically
[root@centos8 bin]# ./podman exec -it 0863497a57ce /bin/bash
bash-4.2# ifconfig
eth0: flags=4163<UP,BROADCAST,RUNNING,MULTICAST>  mtu 1500
        inet 192.168.1.5  netmask 255.255.255.0  broadcast 192.168.1.255
        inet6 fe80::cc5:84ff:fe6a:5498  prefixlen 64  scopeid 0x20<link>
        inet6 **2630:210:f021:9011::3**  prefixlen 64  scopeid 0x0<global>
        ether 0e:c5:84:6a:54:98  txqueuelen 0  (Ethernet)
        RX packets 796  bytes 56417 (55.0 KiB)
        RX errors 0  dropped 166  overruns 0  frame 0
        TX packets 16  bytes 1356 (1.3 KiB)
        TX errors 0  dropped 0 overruns 0  carrier 0  collisions 0

lo: flags=73<UP,LOOPBACK,RUNNING>  mtu 65536
        inet 127.0.0.1  netmask 255.0.0.0
        inet6 ::1  prefixlen 128  scopeid 0x10<host>
        loop  txqueuelen 1000  (Local Loopback)
        RX packets 0  bytes 0 (0.0 B)
        RX errors 0  dropped 0  overruns 0  frame 0
        TX packets 0  bytes 0 (0.0 B)
        TX errors 0  dropped 0 overruns 0  carrier 0  collisions 0

**Describe the results you expected:**
Only IPv4 address should be assigned.

**Output of `podman version`:**

This is the expected behavior. Why do you create a network with a ipv4 and ipv6 subnet but only want one ip assigned.
--ip just set a single static ip, the other subnets will always get the other ips assigned automatically.

Just create a subnet with only ipv4/ipv6 if you want this.

Thanks for the quick response. I think the macvlan can has the ability to support both ipv4 and ipv6 subnet, but customer should be able to decide use one of them or both. The macvlan setting should be independent with the network setting inside the container. What do you think?

We have the following use case:
Customer created dual stack IPv4/IPv6 macvlan, while creating the instance container, they could specify pure IPv4 or IPv6 address, or dual stack in the beginning. Later they can also add IPv4/IPv6 address based on their needed.

The network says what subnets will be used.

You need to create/use different networks for the containers if some should only have ipv4/ipv6 addresses, create a ipv4, ipv6 and dual stack networks and then you can specify --network ipv4 or --network ipv6 to choose the correct subnets.

--ip only sets one static ip all others will always be automatically assigned.

I can potentially see an argument that we should add a `none` option to `--ip` and `--ip6` (and their counterparts in `--net`) to explicitly specify that a container should not acquire an IPv4 or IPv6 address (not specific to macvlan). This would need support with the network tool, which would make it Netavark-only. I would consider it a feature request and not a bug, though.

Why should we make things more complicated for us? First of this would be netavark only and not cni. Second you can already get the same result by using multiple networks. I don't see why a `podman run --ip none` would be better than a `podman run --network ipv6`

Also note that there is nothing in the backend which can differentiate between --ip and --ip6, they all end up in the static ip slice. If we need such functionality it would require bigger changes.

I said that this would make sense as a feature request, not that I had any particular desire to implement it :smile: 

If someone wanted to add support I'd merge it. I don't see the team spending time to add it, though.

[root@centos8 bin]# ./podman run -itd --name=master --network=ens192 --ipv4=192.168.1.5 32572d8c0408 /bin/bash
Error: unknown flag: --ipv4

Can you tell me the exact commands?

How to specify the container to use specific IPv4 or IPv6 network if use docker compose yaml?

Paul said "Why should we make things more complicated for us? First of this would be netavark only and not cni. Second you can already get the same result by using multiple networks. I don't see why a podman run --ip none would be better than a podman run --network ipv6"

What is the exact command for below command by using "podman run --network ipv4" to only specify IPv4 network and leave IPv6 as none? 
================================================================
./podman run -itd --name=master --network=ens192 --ip=192.168.1.5 32572d8c0408 /bin/bash

We are using compose yaml to start the container, how to specify the container to use specific IPv4 or IPv6 network?

You would have 2 separate networks, one with a v4 subnet and one with a v6 subnet, and connect only to the relevant network. This does present major complications for the macvlan backend as you'll need a separate physical interface for each network, but it resolves the problem for typical networks without issue.
You do not need separate interfaces! You can create different macvlan networks with different subnets but still use the same network interface.
This would seem to be a good blog or howto...

So the only solution to use pure IPv4 or IPv6 ip address in container is to create pure IPv4 or IPv6 macvlan, right?
For example, we have a nic1 interface, we can create nic1.1 for pure IPv4 and nic1.2 for pure IPv6, container a can connect nic1.1 for pure IPv4 address and container b can connect to nic1.2 for pure IPv6 address, right?


If we create dual stack macvlan for nic1, the container connected to nic1 will have both IPv4 and IPv6 address, it cannot have pure IPv4 or IPv6 address with current design, right?
